summary: All 75 registry figures generated successfully. Zero generation failures. 30 structural warnings (palette colors outside COLORS dict for new figures, one missing axis label). No physics errors detected. Three new figures (fig_ext_chart, fig_a1_resolution_structure, fig_fk_spectrum) reviewed with special focus. Overall publication quality is high across the entire suite.

new_figures_focus.name: fig_ext_chart
issues: Uses steelblue (#4682B4) not from the project COLORS dict — structural check flagged this. Should use COLORS['Steinhauer'] or similar.
Legend shows only 'trace 0' — not informative; should label the three trace types (h_0 tower, h_1 tower, higher filtration).
The annotations for 'h_0 tower -> Z' and 'h_0 tower -> Z (stem 4)' at the bottom of the chart are small and partially overlap with axis labels.
new_figures_focus.notes: Physics content is correct: Ext chart shows E2 page of Adams SS for ko with dims 1,2,2,2,3,4 matching machine-checked values. Filtration axis is inverted (0 at top, increasing downward) which is the standard convention for Adams charts. Dot colors distinguish generators. Title is informative. Axis labels include 'Stem (t-s)' and 'Filtration (s)' which are correct. Colorblind-accessible (blue/amber/pink dots).

new_figures_focus.name: fig_a1_resolution_structure
issues: Uses steelblue (#4682B4) and gold (#D4A843) outside the project COLORS dict — 7 structural warnings.
The diagram content is squeezed into a thin horizontal band in the middle of the figure, leaving approximately 60% of the vertical space as whitespace. This wastes the 1200x800 canvas.
Differential labels (d_1, d_2, ..., d_5) and the augmentation label (epsilon) float above the boxes but are small relative to the large whitespace.
new_figures_focus.notes: Physics content is correct: shows the minimal free resolution F2 <- P_0 <- P_1 <- ... <- P_5 with ranks 1,2,2,2,3,4 and generator degrees listed in parentheses. The bidiagonal pattern note at bottom ('Sq(1) diagonal, Sq(2,1) superdiagonal — 4-fold periodic via w_1') is accurate. The resolution chain reads left-to-right with arrows correctly pointing from P_{i+1} to P_i. Box labels correctly show homological degree, rank, and generating degrees.

new_figures_focus.name: fig_fk_spectrum
issues: Title is truncated on the right: 'Fidkowski-Kitaev 8-Majorana Spectrum (Machine-Checked' — the closing parenthesis and possibly more text is clipped.
Uses black/blue/red energy level lines — the red line at bottom is not from the standard project palette and could be problematic for red-green colorblind readers.
The figure is not part of the FIGURE_REGISTRY in review_figures.py, so it was not regenerated by the pipeline and has no structural checks.
new_figures_focus.notes: Physics content appears correct: shows energy levels of the 8-Majorana FK model with multiplicities (m=3, m=7, m=4, m=1, m=1). The gap Delta=2 between the two lowest levels is annotated. The multiplicities 3+7+4+1+1=16 which is 2^4 (half of 2^8=256 total Fock space), consistent with even-parity sector. Energy axis labeled. The 'Machine-Checked' tag in the title is important but truncated.

figures.name: fig1_transonic_profiles
figures.notes: Three-panel layout (velocity/sound speed, Mach number, density) for three BEC platforms. Colors are blue/amber/pink — colorblind-safe. All axes labeled with units. Mach=1 sonic horizon clearly marked. Density values positive. Dashed vs solid lines distinguish v(x) and c_s(x). Publication-ready.

figures.name: fig2_correction_hierarchy
issues: Uses red/green bar colors for dissipative/dispersive which may be problematic for red-green colorblind readers. The project standard calls for blue/amber.
figures.notes: Physics correct: correction hierarchy shows dispersive > dissipative > cross for all platforms, with log-scale y-axis showing separation of scales. Values annotated on bars. Title informative.

figures.name: fig3_parameter_space
figures.notes: 2D parameter space with contour fill and three experimental markers. Blue monochrome contour is colorblind-safe. Star markers for experiments clearly visible. Axes labeled with physics quantities. Good use of log scale.

figures.name: fig4_spin_sonic_enhancement
figures.notes: Log-log plot showing quadratic enhancement. Three experimental points marked. Shaded region below curve. Clean rendering. Axes with units.

figures.name: fig5_temperature_decomposition
issues: Some annotation text on the Heidelberg and Trento correction-only panels (bottom row) shows negative values with very small font that may be hard to read at publication size.
The red bars dominate visually while the black T_H bars are the physically dominant term — consider using the project blue/amber palette.
figures.notes: Physics correct: T_H dominates for all platforms. Correction terms shown in separate bottom panels for clarity. Six-panel layout is information-dense but readable.

figures.name: fig6_kappa_scaling
issues: Uses green and red lines — not colorblind-accessible. Should be blue/amber per project convention.
figures.notes: Physics correct: dispersive correction scales as kappa^2, dissipative as 1/kappa, crossing point clearly marked. Log-log scaling appropriate. Three experimental platforms marked as vertical lines.

figures.name: fig7_cgl_fdr_pattern
figures.notes: Bar chart showing monomial counting at each EFT order. Three bar types (dissipative/conservative/noise) with formula line overlay. Clean rendering. Correct count pattern floor((N+1)/2)+1.

figures.name: fig8_even_vs_odd_kernel
figures.notes: Four-panel layout demonstrating CGL FDR. Even kernel symmetric, odd kernel antisymmetric, noise from odd only. Green/red colors used but within physics convention for conservative/dissipative decomposition. Summary bar chart (panel d) is clear.

figures.name: fig9_boundary_term_suppression
figures.notes: IBP boundary correction shown as O(D). All experiments well below 2% threshold. EFT breakdown boundary marked. Log-log scaling appropriate. Shaded region guides the eye.

figures.name: fig10_positivity_constraint
figures.notes: Positivity constraint region clearly shown with green allowed band and strict line through origin. Axis labels use gamma notation. Clean rendering.

figures.name: fig11_on_shell_vanishing
figures.notes: On-shell vanishing at k=omega/c_s clearly demonstrated. Zero crossing marked with dashed vertical line and shaded region. Axis labels correct. Physics key finding displayed well.

figures.name: fig12_einstein_relation
figures.notes: Three linear curves for different gamma values showing sigma=gamma*T. Colors (red/tan/amber) are reasonably distinguishable. CGL master formula annotation present. Clean rendering.

figures.name: fig13_parity_alternation
figures.notes: Heatmap-style chart showing monomial structure by (N, m) quantum numbers. Blue/amber colors used correctly for universal/flow-only. All cells labeled with (N,m) coordinates. Readable.

figures.name: fig14_parity_alternation_stakeholder
figures.notes: Bar chart version of parity alternation for wider audience. Blue/amber colors correct. Formula overlay count(N)=floor((N+1)/2)+1. Clean and readable.

figures.name: fig15_damping_rate_third_order
figures.notes: Two-panel layout: damping rate convergence (left) and fractional correction (right). Orders 1-3 shown. Log-log scaling. Curves nearly overlap showing convergence. Clean.

figures.name: fig16_spectral_correction_comparison
figures.notes: Two-panel layout comparing delta^(2) (odd in omega) and delta^(3) (even in omega). Parity properties clearly visible. On-shell vanishing noted for delta^(2). Clean rendering.

figures.name: fig17_kappa_crossing
figures.notes: Six curves (3 platforms x 2 corrections) on log-log plot. Dashed vs solid distinguish dispersive/dissipative. Blue/amber/magenta platform colors are colorblind-accessible. Crossing point marked.

figures.name: fig18_spin_sonic_phase3
figures.notes: Three platform curves showing spin-sonic enhancement. Log-log scaling. Blue/magenta/amber colors. Clean rendering with no overlapping labels.

figures.name: fig19_bogoliubov_connection
figures.notes: Two-panel layout: dispersion relation and k^4 deviation. Acoustic vs Bogoliubov clearly distinguished. Fractional deviation growing as expected at high k. Clean.

figures.name: fig20_sm_scorecard
issues: Uses red bars for 'ERASED' groups — convention calls for amber/blue. Red/green adjacent (red erased vs green survives for U(1)_Y) is not ideal for colorblind readers.
figures.notes: Physics correct: SU(3)_c and SU(2)_L erased (non-abelian center), U(1)_Y survives. Center subgroup annotations present. Clear status labels.

figures.name: fig21_erasure_survey
issues: Uses predominantly red bars for 'Erased' groups — same red/green palette concern.
X-axis tick labels are getting crowded with many gauge groups — some may overlap at smaller display sizes.
figures.notes: Physics correct: all SU(N>1) and SO(N>1) erased, only U(1) survives. Boundary markers for SU(N=SO(N)) and SO(N=U(1)) shown. Comprehensive survey of gauge groups.

figures.name: fig22_complex_turning_point
figures.notes: Three platform curves showing imaginary shift vs frequency. Blue/magenta/amber colors are colorblind-safe. Log-y scale. Axes correctly labeled. Clean.

figures.name: fig23_effective_surface_gravity
figures.notes: Three-panel layout (one per platform) with dispersive/dissipative decomposition. Green/red colors used for disp/diss, but curves are well-separated. Log-y scale. Subplot titles clear.

figures.name: fig24_decoherence_and_noise
figures.notes: Two-panel layout: decoherence parameter and FDR noise floor. Three platforms in blue/magenta/amber. Log-y scale. Clear axes and subplot titles.

figures.name: fig25_hawking_spectrum_exact
figures.notes: Three vertically stacked panels showing full Hawking spectrum per platform with total, Planck, and noise components. Line styles distinguish components. Log-y scale. Publication-ready.

figures.name: fig26_hawking_spectrum_stakeholder
figures.notes: Overlay of all three platforms. Nearly identical curves at low omega, diverging at high omega. Blue/magenta/amber. Clean single-panel layout for stakeholder presentation.

figures.name: fig27_exact_vs_perturbative
figures.notes: Fractional difference plot. Heidelberg shows significant deviation at high omega. Steinhauer/Trento remain small. Clean rendering. Y-axis label correctly describes the quantity.

figures.name: fig28_adw_effective_potential
figures.notes: Three coupling ratios showing effective potential. Dashed/dotted/solid line styles. Green/black/red color scheme. Critical coupling shows transition from convex to non-convex. Physics correct.

figures.name: fig29_adw_phase_diagram
figures.notes: Phase diagram with tetrad VEV vs coupling. Pre-geometric and full tetrad phases labeled. Critical coupling G_c marked. Shaded region. Clean and informative.

figures.name: fig30_adw_ng_modes
figures.notes: Vergeles mode decomposition bar chart. Four categories (Absorbed=6, Massless Graviton=2, Massive Higgs=4, Pure Gauge=4). Total 16=4^2 annotated. Clean and correct.

figures.name: fig31_adw_he3_analogy
issues: Text-heavy diagram with significant whitespace. Content is squeezed into a relatively narrow vertical band. Could benefit from better vertical space utilization.
figures.notes: Structural comparison between ADW tetrad and He-3 superfluid is correct: order parameter, symmetry, NG absorbed modes, massless modes, massive modes all matched. Two-column layout is clear.

figures.name: fig32_adw_obstacles
figures.notes: Horizontal bar chart showing four structural obstacles with severity (Moderate/Serious). Blue/amber colors used. Status 'All Open' noted. Clean and informative.

figures.name: fig33_adw_coupling_scan
figures.notes: Two-panel layout: potential depth and tetrad VEV vs coupling for N_f=2,4,8. Blue/magenta/amber colors. G_c marked. Physics shows deeper potential and higher VEV for more fermion species. Correct.

figures.name: fig34_adw_coupling_scan_stakeholder
figures.notes: Stakeholder version with simplified labels ('2 fermion species' etc). Same content as fig33 with clearer labeling for non-specialist audience. Clean.

figures.name: fig35_prediction_table_comparison
figures.notes: Spectral deviation from Planckian for three platforms. Heidelberg shows dramatic deviation at high omega. Steinhauer/Trento very small. Blue/magenta/amber. Clean.

figures.name: fig36_detector_requirements
figures.notes: Bar chart: shots needed for three measurement goals. Current capability (7000 shots) marked as horizontal line. Blue/magenta/amber platform colors. Log scale appropriate. Shows WKB vs EFT needs far fewer shots.

figures.name: fig37_kappa_scaling
figures.notes: Two-panel kappa-scaling test: dispersive (D^2 confirmed) and dissipative (D-independent confirmed). Green/red lines but well-separated panels. Physics correct: power-law scaling verified.

figures.name: fig38_noise_floor_crossover
figures.notes: Hawking vs noise floor crossover. Six curves (3 platforms x 2 quantities). Solid/dashed distinguish Hawking/noise. Crossing points marked. Log-y scale. Clean and physically informative.

figures.name: fig39_chirality_wall_status
figures.notes: Four GS conditions shown with status (Evaded/Applies/Unclear). Blue/amber/grey color coding. Clean bar chart format. Labels readable.

figures.name: fig40_gl_phase_diagram
figures.notes: Ginzburg-Landau free energy for B-phase, A-phase, polar phases. Blue/magenta/amber/grey. G_c marked. B-phase lowest energy (ground state). Correct He-3 analogy.

figures.name: fig41_he3_comparison_table
issues: Table rendered as a figure — text is quite small and may be hard to read at column width. Check marks are tiny.
figures.notes: Comprehensive structural comparison table with 12+ rows. All matches and mismatches correctly annotated. Content accurate per He-3/ADW analogy.

figures.name: fig42_vestigial_phase_diagram
figures.notes: Two-panel phase diagram: order parameters and phase classification. Tetrad VEV and metric correlator shown. Phase boundary at G_c correct. Clean rendering.

figures.name: fig43_backreaction_cooling
figures.notes: Cooling curves for three platforms. Steinhauer shows strongest cooling. Heidelberg/Trento plateau at different asymptotic values. Blue/magenta/amber. Time axis in units of tau_cool. Clean.

figures.name: fig44_information_retention
figures.notes: Bar chart: standard hydro vs fracton (dipole/quadrupole) conserved charges by dimension. Fracton retains more information as dimension increases. Blue/teal/amber. Clean and physically correct.

figures.name: fig45_vestigial_effective_potential
figures.notes: Effective potential for three coupling ratios (pre-geometric/vestigial/full tetrad). Green/amber/blue. Shows SSB transition correctly. Clean axis labels.

figures.name: fig46_kappa_scaling_physical
figures.notes: Physical kappa-scaling: six curves (3 platforms x 2 corrections). Dashed/solid distinguish types. Log-log scaling. Blue/magenta/amber platform colors. Clean.

figures.name: fig48_grassmann_trg_2d_phase
figures.notes: Two vertically stacked panels: free energy density and specific heat. g*=0 critical point marked. Specific heat shows expected peak at transition. Clean rendering.

figures.name: fig49_fermion_bag_4d_binder
figures.notes: 4D Binder cumulants for metric and tetrad. Both near 2/3 (ordered phase) across coupling range. Dashed ordered/disordered reference lines. Blue/amber. Clean.

figures.name: fig50_fermion_bag_4d_phase_diagram
figures.notes: Two-panel: connected metric correlator and MC acceptance rate. Both show smooth variation. Blue/amber. Clean axis labels. Acceptance rate decline at strong coupling is physical.

figures.name: fig51_vestigial_binder_crossing
issues: 20 structural warnings for #666666 color not in COLORS dict.
Legend shows L=4 through L=16 — very crowded with 13 line entries. Many lines overlap making individual L values hard to distinguish.
The Binder cumulant range shown (0.6640-0.6670 for tetrad) is extremely narrow — the y-axis barely spans 0.003 units, making it hard to see if there is genuine crossing behavior or just noise.
figures.notes: Physics content shows Binder cumulants for many lattice sizes. The extremely narrow y-range and overlapping curves suggest the crossing point is not cleanly resolved with current statistics. Left panel (tetrad) and right panel (metric) both shown.

figures.name: fig52_vestigial_susceptibility_split
figures.notes: Split transition: tetrad vs metric susceptibility peaks at different couplings. Blue/magenta bars. Split Delta=0.35 annotated. Multiple lattice sizes L=4 through L=16 shown.

figures.name: fig53_gs_condition_formalization
figures.notes: GS no-go conditions C1-C6, I1-I3 with formalization status (Substantive/Axiom) and TPF violation markers. Purple/amber/magenta colors. Legend explains categories. Comprehensive overview.

figures.name: fig54_lean_theorem_summary
figures.notes: Stacked bar chart: manual vs Aristotle proofs per module. Blue/amber. 10 modules shown. Clear relative proportions. Publication-ready.

figures.name: fig55_category_hierarchy
figures.notes: Three bars showing Mathlib existing (9), Wave 4A new (5), Wave 4B new (5). Structure names listed on bars. Blue/magenta/amber. Clean.

figures.name: fig56_fusion_rules_comparison
figures.notes: Three heatmaps: Vec_Z2, Rep_S3, Fibonacci. Fusion multiplicities shown as numbers in cells. Blue monochrome colorscale. Correct fusion rules displayed.

figures.name: fig57_fibonacci_f_matrix
figures.notes: 2x2 F-matrix heatmap with golden ratio entries (0.7862, -0.6180, 0.6180, 0.7862). Blue-red diverging colorscale. Correct Fibonacci anyon F-matrix. Clean.

figures.name: fig58_drinfeld_anyon_spectrum
figures.notes: Two-panel: number of anyons and algebra dimension for three Drinfeld doubles. Blue/magenta/amber. Correct values: D(Z/2)=4 anyons/dim 4, D(Z/3)=9/9, D(S3)=8/36. Clean.

figures.name: fig59_layer123_bridge
figures.notes: Three-layer architecture diagram. Layer 3 (EFT/Hydro), Layer 2 (Gauge Theory), Layer 1 (Categorical). Blue/magenta/amber. Theorem counts and connecting arrows. Clean and informative.

figures.name: fig60_tpf_evasion_architecture
figures.notes: Five GS conditions violated by TPF construction. Each bar shows condition name, mechanism, and Lean theorem name. Purple/amber colors. Clean.

figures.name: fig61_fock_exterior_algebra
figures.notes: Bar chart: dim(FermionicFockSpace k) = 2^k for k=1..8. Blue bars with amber 2^k reference line. Log-y scale. Values annotated. Aristotle proof cited. Clean.

figures.name: fig62_vestigial_phase_diagram_mc
figures.notes: Mean-field curves overlaid with MC susceptibility peak markers. Blue/amber. MC diamonds at expected locations. Vestigial boundary shaded. Clean.

figures.name: fig63_vestigial_susceptibility
figures.notes: RPA metric susceptibility for four u_g values. Curves show divergence near G_ves. Zero-crossing defines vestigial transition. Clean rendering with subtle color gradient.

figures.name: fig64_vestigial_window
figures.notes: G_ves/G_c ratio vs u_g for trace and traceless-symmetric channels. BCS-like scaling annotation. Blue solid and amber dashed. Clean.

figures.name: fig65_vestigial_phase_diagram_analytical
figures.notes: Analytical phase diagram: pre-geometric, vestigial, Einstein-Cartan phases. Red/blue boundaries. Vestigial phase shaded. Phase labels readable. Publication-ready.

figures.name: fig66_gt_band_structure
figures.notes: GT BdG band structure along high-symmetry path. Valence (blue) and conduction (magenta) bands. Weyl node at Gamma clearly marked. Single gapless point. Correct physics.

figures.name: fig67_wilson_mass_bz
figures.notes: Wilson mass heatmap in BZ. Zero only at origin (Weyl node) marked with amber dot. Blue monochrome colorscale. Clean. Single zero demonstrates no doubling.

figures.name: fig68_chiral_charge_spectrum
figures.notes: Two-panel: chiral charge eigenvalues +/-cos(p3/2) and Ginsparg-Wilson norm cos^2(p3/2). Blue/magenta for eigenvalues, green for norm. Non-compact spectrum shown correctly.

figures.name: fig69_gt_commutator_verification
figures.notes: Scatter plot: [H,Q_A] commutator entries at machine epsilon (~10^-16) for all 512 k-points. Red dashed line at 10^-15 threshold. All points well below. Numerical verification convincing.

figures.name: fig70_chirality_wall_three_pillars
figures.notes: Three-column architectural diagram: No-Go (GS conditions), GT Construction, Anomaly. Each pillar shows key results and theorem counts. Bottom summary shows total: 968 theorems, 0 axioms, 66 modules, zero sorry. Clean and comprehensive.

figures.name: fig71_sm_fermion_z16_anomaly
issues: The nu_R bar label is rendered vertically and partially overlaps with the Z16 boundary annotation at the right edge.
The 'Without nu_R (15)' and Z16 boundary (16) annotations are close together in the upper right.
figures.notes: Physics correct: cumulative bars reach 16 with nu_R (6+3+3+2+1+1=16). Blue/amber/green colors from project palette. The Z16 classification is correctly represented.

figures.name: fig72_sm_generation_anomaly
figures.notes: Z16 anomaly index vs generation count. With nu_R: always 0 mod 16 (blue flat line). Without nu_R: decreasing residue (amber diamonds). N_f=3 observed value marked. Clean and correct.

figures.name: fig73_sm_generation_constraint
figures.notes: Chiral central charge c_-=8*N_f with mod 24 grid lines. N_f must be multiple of 3 for c_-=0 mod 24. Bar chart with values annotated. Blue/grey colors. Clean.

figures.name: fig74_drinfeld_equivalence_structure
issues: Structural check warning: xaxis title missing expected 'anyon' label.
Left panel (toric code) has a red annotation marker that appears misplaced — it sits below the 'e (electric)' bar but is labeled 'e (m) -> e' which is confusing.
figures.notes: Physics correct: Z(Vec_{Z/2}) shows 4 anyons all with d=1 (toric code). Z(Vec_{S3}) shows 8 anyons with mixed dimensions d=1,2,3. D^2 values annotated. Blue/magenta/amber columns.

non_registry_figures.name: fig_rhmc_l8_preliminary
issues: Title explicitly says 'PRELIMINARY ... not publication-quality' — this is correct, but the figure should be clearly marked as not for publication.
Small figure size makes axis labels hard to read.
Footer text is truncated/overlapping.
non_registry_figures.notes: Four-panel RHMC analysis: observables vs coupling. Preliminary data with amber diamonds and blue circles. Acceptance rate issues at low g noted. Correctly marked as preliminary.

cross_cutting_issues.issue: Several early figures (fig2, fig5, fig6, fig8, fig20, fig21, fig23) use red/green color pairs that are not ideal for colorblind readers. The project standard is blue/amber. These predate the Phase 3+ figures which consistently use the correct palette.
cross_cutting_issues.recommendation: Consider updating Phase 1-2 figures to use the project COLORS dict (blue/amber) in a future cleanup pass.

cross_cutting_issues.issue: New figures fig_ext_chart and fig_a1_resolution_structure use steelblue (#4682B4) and gold (#D4A843) which are not in the project COLORS dict, causing 9 structural warnings.
cross_cutting_issues.recommendation: Map these figures to use the project COLORS dict entries for consistency.

cross_cutting_issues.issue: fig51_vestigial_binder_crossing uses #666666 (grey) for many traces, causing 20 structural warnings. The legend has 13 entries (L=4 through L=16) which is very crowded.
cross_cutting_issues.recommendation: Reduce to representative lattice sizes (L=4,8,16) or use a gradient from the project palette.

cross_cutting_issues.issue: fig_a1_resolution_structure has significant vertical whitespace — the resolution chain occupies only about 30% of the canvas height.
cross_cutting_issues.recommendation: Adjust figure aspect ratio or increase the vertical extent of the diagram elements.

cross_cutting_issues.issue: fig_fk_spectrum title is truncated. It is also not in the FIGURE_REGISTRY.
cross_cutting_issues.recommendation: Add to FIGURE_REGISTRY and fix title rendering (reduce font size or abbreviate).
